l.seg: pad nām ī dādār ī weh abzōnīg
In the name of the beneficient bountiful Creator
pad nām ud nērōg ud ayārīh ī dādār Ohrmazd ǰadag ī nēk.
In the name and power and righteousness of the Creator Ohrmazd and good omen.

s.seg: šahrestānīhā ī andar zamīg ī Ērān-šahr kerd estēd ǰud ǰud būm kū kadām sar-xwadāy kerd pad gōkān abar ēn ayādgār nibišt estēd.
The cities in the land of Ērān-šahr which have been built in different days, where and which overlord made them is written in detail in this memoir.
pad kust ī xwarāsān samarkand šahrestān kay-us ī kawādān bun fragand siyāwaxš ī kay-usān bē frazāmēnīd.
In the Eastern direction, the foundation of the city of Samarkand was laid by Kāus, the son of Kawād. Sīyāwaxš, the son of Kāus completed it.
kay-husrō ī siyāwaxšān ānōh zād u-š warzāwand ātaxš wahrām ānōh nišāst.
Kay Husraw, the son of Sīyāwaxš was born there , and he set the miraculous Wahrām fire there.
pas Zardušt dēn āwurd az framān ī wištāsp-šā hazār ud dō-sad fragard pad dēn-dibīrīh pad taxtagīhā ī zarrēn kand ud nibišt ud pad ganǰī ān ātaxš nihād.

Then Zoroaster brought the Religion. By the order of king Wištāsp 1200 fragard (chapters) in the script of religious scripture were engraved on golden tablets and written and deposited in the treasury of that fire-(temple).
ud pas gizistag skandar sōxt ud andar ō drayāb abgand.
And then the accursed Alexander burnt and threw it into the sea .

sugd ī haft āšyān u-š haft āšyānīh ēd kū haft āšyān andar būd: ēk ān ī J̌am ud ēk ān ī azdahāg ud ēk ānī Frēdōn ud ēk ān ī manūčihr ud ēk ān ī kay-us ud ēk ān ī kay-husrō ud ēk ān ī luhrāsp ud ēk ān ī wištāsp-šā.

Sogdiana (has) seven abodes, its having seven abodes in it means that there were seven Lords in it. One belonging to Jam and one to Aži Dahāg and one to Frēdōn and one to Manūčihr and one to Kāus and one to Lohrāsp and one to king Wištāsp.
pas gizistag frāsiyāg ī tūr harw ēk nišēmag ī šāhān uzdes-zār-ēw bašn padiš kdrd.
Then the accursed Frāsiyāk, the Tūrānian, made seats for each of the demons, and an idol temple and a heathen temple.
andar baxl ī bāmīg šahrestānī nawāzag spandyād ī wištāspān kerd.
In the brilliant Balx, the city of Nawāzag was built by Spandyād, the son of Wištāsp.

u-š warzāwand ātaxš wahrām ānōh nišāst u-š nēzag ī xwēš ānōh bē zad u-š ō yabbū-xāgān ud sinǰēbīk-xāgān ud čōl xāgān ud wazurg-xān ud gōhram ud tuzāb ud arzāsp ī xyōnān-šā paygām frēstīd kū nēzag ī man bē nigerēd harw kē pad wēzišn ī ēn nēzag nigerēd čē andar ō Ērān-šahr dwārēd.

And he set the miraculous Wahrām fire there and struck his lance there and he sent a message to Yabbu Xāgān, Sinjēbik Xāgān, and Čōl Xāgān and the Great Xāgān and Gohram and Tuzāb and Arzāsp, the king of the Hayōns: “behold my lance, whoever beholds the movement of this lance is like they have rushed to Iran.”
šahrestān ī xwārazm narsēh ī yahūdagān kerd.
The city of Xwārazm was built by Narsēh, the son of the Jewess.
šahrestān ī marw-rōd wahrām ī yazdagirdān kerd.
The city of Marv-rōd was built by Wahrām, the son of Yazdgerd.
šahrestān ī marw ud sahrestānī harē gizistag skandar ī harōmīg kerd.
The city of Marv and the city of Herāt were built by the accursed Alexander the Roman.
šahrestānī pōšang šāpuhr ī ardašīrān kerd u-š pad pōšang puhl-ē-wazurg kerd.
The city of Pōšang was built by Šābuhr the son of Ardaxšīr, and he built a large bridge in Pōšang.
šahrestānī tūs tūs ī nōdarān [kerd ī] nōh-sad sāl spāhbed būd. pas az tūs spāhbedīh ō zarēr ud az zarēr ō bastwar ud az bastwar ō karazm mad.
The city of Tūs was built by Tūs, the son of Nōdar, and (he) was the general for 900 years. After Tūs, the office of general came to Zarēr and from Zarēr to Bastūr and from Bastūr to Karzam.
šahrestān ī nēw-šāpuhr šāpuhr ī ardašīrān kerd pad ān gāh ka-š pāhlēzag ī tūr ōzad u-š pad ham gyāg šahrestān-ē framūd kerdan.
The city of Nēwšābuhr was built by Šābuhr the son of Ardaxšīr. At that time when he killed the Tūrānian Pahlīzag, he ordered to build the city in the same place.
šahrestān ī kāyēn kay-luhrāsp ī wištāsp pid kerd.
The city of Qāyēn was built by Kay Lohrāsp, the father of Wištāsp.
andar gurgān šahrestān ī dahestān xwānēnd narsēh ī aškānān kerd.
In Gurgān, the city which they call Dahestān was built by Narseh, the Arsacid.
šahrestān ī kūmisud panǰ burg azdahāg pad šabestān kerd mānišnī pārsīgān ānōh būd pad xwadāyīh yazdgird ī šāpuhrān kerd andar tāzišn ī čōl nērōg-ē pāhr ān ālag.

The city of Kūmis of five towers Aži Dahāg made it his own harem. The abode of the *Parthians was there. In the reign of Yazdgird, the son of Šābuhr made it during the invasion of the Čōl, at the boundary of the Gruznian Guard.
šahrestān ī panǰ-husrō:šād-husrō must-ābād-husrō ud wisp-šād-husrō ud hu-bōy-husrō šād-farrox-husrō kawādān kay-husrō kerd ud nām nihād.
Five cities were built by Husraw, the son of Kawād: Husraw-šād, and Husraw ī Mūst-Ābād, and Wisp-šād-Husraw and Hū-bōy-Husraw, and Šād-farrox-Husraw and named them.
u-š frasp-ēw kē ē sad ud haštād frasang drahnāy ud wīst ud panǰ šāh-ārešn bālā ē sad ud haštād dar kōškud dastgird andar framūd kerdan.
He ordered the establishment of a wall 180 farsang(s) long and 25 royal cubit(s) high, 180 palace gates and ordered in it the establishment of an estate.
pad kustī xwarwarān šahrestān ī tīsifōn az framān ī tūs warāzag ī gēwagān kerd.
In the Western direction, the city of Ctesiphon was built by the order of Tūs, the son of Warāz from the family of Gēw.
šahrestān ī nasībīn warāzagī gēwagān kerd.
The city of Nisibis was built by Warāzag the son of Gēw.
šahrestān ī ōrhāy narsēh ī aškānān kerd.
The city of Edessa was built by Narseh, the Arsacid.
šahrestān ī bābēl pad xwadāyīhī J̌am kerd u-š tīr abāxtar ānōh bē bast pad mārīgī haft ud dwāzdah axtarān ud abāxtarān ud haštom bārag pad ǰādūgīh ō mihr azērīg bē nimūd.

The city of Babylon was built by Bābēl during the reign of Jam. And he bound the planet Mercury there and he showed the seven planets and the twelve constellations and the eighth portions by sorcery to the Sun and to those below (mankind).
šahrestān ī hērat šāpuhr ī ardašīrān kerd u-š mihr-zād ī hērat marz-bān pad war ī tāzīgān bē gumārd.
The city of Hīra was built by Šābuhr, the son of Ardaxšīr and he appointed Mihrzād the margrave of Hīra over the wall of the Arabs.
šahrestān ī hamadān yazdgird ī šāpuhrān kerd kē-šān yazdgird ī dabar xwānēnd.
The city of Hamadān was built by Yazdgird, the son of Šābuhr whom they call Yazdgird the rough.
andar māh ud kust ī nihāwand ud warī wahrām-āwand šahrestān-ēw wahrām ī yazdgirdān kerd kē-šān wahrām gōr xwānd.
In Media and the district of Nihāwand and the fortress of Wahrāmāwand, Wahrām the son of Yazdgird whom they call Wahrām Gōr, built a city.
wīst-ēw šahrestān ī andar padišxwārgar kerd estēd čē ēg-iš az framān ī armāyīl awēšān kōfyārān kerd kē-šān az azdahāg kōf pad šahryārīh windād estād.
21 cities were built in Padišxwārgar, either Armāyīl or by the order of Armāyīl were built by the mountaineers, who from Aži Dahāg acquired the dominion of the mountains.
kōfyār haft hēnd pad dumbāwand: wisēmagān ud āhagān ud wispūr ud sōbārān ud musragān ud barōzān ud marinzān.
There are seven mountaineers: Wisēmagān of Damāwand, and Āhagān and Wispūhr and Sōbārān and Musragān and Barōzān and Marinzān.
ēn ān būd kē-šān az azdahāg kōf pad šahryārīh windād estād.
These were those who from Aži Dahāg acquired the dominion of the mountains.
šahrestān mūsel pērōz šāpuhrān kerd.
The city of Mosul was built by Pērōz, the son of Šābuhr.
nōh šahrestān andar zamīg gazīrag kerd estēd āmitūs kēsar brādar-zād kerd.
9 cities were built in the land of Jazīra, and Āmtūs, the nephew of Caesar built them.
wīst čahār šahrestān andar zamīg šām ud frīgā ud kufah ud makkah ud madīnag gāh kerd estēd ast šāhān šāh ast kēsar.
The 24 cities which were built in the land of Syria and Yemen and Africa and Kufa and Mecca and Medina, some were built by the King of Kings and (some built) by the Caesar.
pad kust nēmrōz šahrestān ī kābul ardašīr ī spandyādān kerd estēd.
In the Southern direction, the city of Kābul was built by Ardaxšīr, the son of Spandyād.
šahrestān raxwat rahām ī gōdarzān kerd pad ān gāh ka-š asp warz-bar ī tūr ōzad u-š yabbū-xāgān az ānōh stō bē kerd.
The city of Raxvat was built by Rahām, the son of Gōdarz, at that time when he, Aspwarz killed the manly Tūr, and harassed Yabbū the Xāgān from there.
šahrestān ī bost bastwar ī zarērān kerd pad ān gāh ka wištasp-šā dēn yaštan pad frazdān būd ud bunag ī wištāsp ud abārīg wāspuhragān andar nišāst.
The city of Bust was built by Bastūr, the son of Zarēr, at that time when king Wištāsp worshipped the religion by the Frazdān (lake), and the abode of Wištāsp and other blood princes was established.
šahrestān ī frāh ud šahrestān zāwalestān rōdstahm ī sīstān-šā kerd.
The city of Frāh and the city of Zābul were built by Rustam, the king of Sīstān.

šahrestān zarang naxust gizistag frāsiyāg ī tūr kerd u-š warzāwand ātaxš karkōy ānōh nišāst u-š manuščihr andar ō padišxwārgar kerd u-š Spandarmad pad zanīh xwāst ud Spandarmad andar ō zamīg gumēxt šahrestān awērān kerd u-š ātaxš afsārd ud pas kay-hu-sraw siyāwašān šahrestān abāz kerd u-š ātaxš karkōy abāz nišāt ud ardašīr bābagān šahrestān bun pad frazāmēnīd.

The city of Zarang was first built by the accursed Frāsiyāk, the Tūrānian, and established the miraculous Karkōy Fire there, and Manūčihr was (surrounded) in Padišxwargar, and (Frāsiyāk) asked Spandarmad as wife and Spandarmad mixed in the earth, (he) destroyed the city and he extinguished the fire, and then Kay Husraw, the son of Sīyāwaxš again built the city. And he again founded the Karkōy Fire, and Ardaxšīr, the son of Pābag finished the city.
šahrestān kirmān pērōzān kirmān-šā kerd.
The city of Kermān was built by (*Kawād) the son of Pērōz, the king of Kermān.
šahrestān weh-ardašīr pad sē xwadāy kerd ud ardašīr bābagān pad frazāmēnīd.
The city of Weh-Ardaxšīr was built by three lords and Ardaxšīr, the son of Pābag finished it.
šahrestān staxr ardawān ī pārsīgān-šā kerd.
The city of Staxr was built by Ardawān, king of the *Parthians.
šahrestān dārāb-kird dārāy ī dārāyān kerd.
The city of Dārābjird was built by Dārā, the son of Dārā.
šahrestān weh-šāpuhr šāpuhr ī ardāšīrān kerd.
The city of Bēšābuhr was built by Šābuhr, the son of Ardaxšīr.
šahrestān gōr-ardasīr-xwarrah ardāšīr bābagān kerd.
The city of Gōr-Ardaxšīr-Xwarrah was built by Ardaxšīr, the son of Pābag.
šahrestān tawwag humāy ī čihr-āzādān kerd.
The city of Tūz was built by Humāy, the son of Čihr Āzād.
šahrestān ī Ohrmazd ardašīrān ud šahrestān ī rām-Ohrmazd Ohrmazd nēw šāphrān kerd.
The city of Hormizd-Ardaxšīr and the city of Rām-Hormizd were built by Hormizd, the valiant, the son of Šābuhr.
šahrestān šūs ud šūstar šīšīnduxt zan ī yazdgird šāpuhrān kerd čiyōn duxt ī rēš-galūdag yahūdagān-šā mād-iz ī wahrām ī gōr būd.
The city of Susa and Šūštar were built by Šīšinduxṭ, the wife of Yazdgird, the son of Šābuhr, since she was the daughter of Reš Galut, the king of the Jews and also was the mother of Wahrām Gōr.
šahrestān ī wandōy-šāpuhr ud šahrestān ī ērān-kerd-šāpuhr šapuhr ī ardašīrān kerd. u-š palābād nām nihād.
The city of Wandōy-Šābuhr and the city of Ērān-kard-Šābuhr, Šābuhr, the son of Ardaxšīr built, and he named it Pīlābad.
šahrestān ī nahr-tīrag pad duš-xwadāyīh ī azdahāg pad šabestān kerd ud zēndān ī Ērān-šahr būd zēndān aškān nām būd.
The city of Nahr-Tīrag was built during the evil reign of Aži Dahāg as a harem, and was the prison of Ērān-šahr, and it was called the prison of the Arsac.

šahrestān simrān Frēdōn ī ādwēnān kerd u-š maswar.simrān-šā ōzad u-š zamīg ī simrān abāz xwēsīh Ērān šahr āwurd u-š dašt tāzīg pad xwēsīh ud ābādīh bē ō baxt-hu-sraw ī tāzīg-šā dād paywand dārišn ī xwēš ray.

The city of Himyār was built by Frēdōn, the son of Ādwēn. And he killed Masrugh, the king of Himyār, and he again brought the land of Himyār under the sovereignty of Ērān-šahr. And he gave the plain of the Arabs to Baxt Husraw, the king of the Arabs as his very own feud, on the account of the connection which he himself made with him.
šhrestān ī ārēst šāpuhr ī ardašīrān kerd.
The city of Ārhēst was built by Šābuhr, the son of Ardaxšīr.
šahrestān ī asūr ud šahrestān ī weh-ardašīr [ardašīr] ī spandyādān kerd. u-š ōšag ī hagar marzbān gund-gar ud burg-gar pad war ī tāzīgān bē gumārd.
The city of Āsūr and the city of Weh-Ardaxšīr were built by Ardaxšīr, the son of Spandyād. And he appointed Ōšag, of Hagar as the margrave (over the) Dō-sar and Bor-gil by the wall of the Arabs.
šahrestān ī gay gizistag aleksandar ī flīpān kerd mānīšn ī yahūdān ānōh būd ī pad xwadāyīh ī yazdgird ī šāpuhrān nīd az xwāhišn ī šīšīnduxt ī-š zan būd .

The city of Gay was built by the accursed Alexander, the son of Philip. The dwelling of the Jews was there. During the reign of Yazdgird, the son of Šābuhr, (the Jews) were led there by the request of Šīšinduxṭ who was his wife.
šahrestān ī ērān-āsān-kerd-kawād kawād pērōzan kerd.
The city of Ērān-āsān-kard-Kawād was built by (*Kawād), the son of Pērōz.
šahrestān ī aškar wahrām ī yazdgirdān kerd.
The city of Aškar was built by Wahrām, the son of Yazdgird.
šahrestān ī ādurbādagān ērān-gušnasp ī ādurbādagān spāhbed [kerd].
The city of Ādurbādagān was (built) by Ērān-Gušasp who was the general of Ādurbādagān.
šahrestān ī wan wan ī gulaxšān kerd ud pad zanīh ō kay-kawād mad ud drubuštīh arwand-asp ī tūr ī brād-rōš ī karb pad ǰādūgīh kerd pānagīh ī gyān xwēš rāy.

The city of Wan was built by Wan, the daughter of Gulaxšān and became married to Kay Kawād, and the fortification of Arwandāsp was built by Tūr, the son Brātrēš the Karp by sorcery as a place of protection for his own life.
pad kust ādurbādagān šahrestān ī ganzag frāsiyāg tūr kerd.
In the direction of Ādurbādagān , the City of Ganzag was built by Frāsiyāk, the son of Tūr.
šahrestān ī āmōγ zandīg ī purr-marg kerd. Zardušt ī Spitāmān az ān šahrestān būd.
The city of Āmol was built by the heretic who is full of death. Zoroaster, the son of Spitāmān was from that city.
šahrestān ī bagdād abū-gāfar čiyōn-šān abū-dawānīg xwānd kerd.
The city of Baghdad was built by Abū Jaʿfar whom they call Abū Dawāniq.

lg.l: . frazaft pad drōd šādīh rāmišn. Finished with prosperity, happiness and peace.
